Verified all four share/agents/*.md are byte-identical to .claude/agents/*.md minus exactly the 'skills:' front-matter line, and the drift gate (whole-file equality after stripping only that line) plus the assert.match on the dogfood copy makes the strip non-vacuous. Note the strip regex /^skills:.*\n/m removes only the FIRST match anywhere in the file; safe today because the line exists only in front-matter, but a future body line starting with 'skills:' would confuse the gate.

Criterion 2's write-if-absent at init time is only proven indirectly: init refuses on an existing spine, so the survives-re-run test exercises `chalk agents --claude` instead. installClaudeAgents is the single shared code path for both, so coverage is real, but a direct unit test of installClaudeAgents(root) with a pre-existing file would pin the guarantee independent of CLI routing.

The tarball test shells out to `npm pack --dry-run --json` (120s timeout); it is the right assertion for the ENOENT-for-npm-users risk and package.json files[] now includes 'share', but it introduces an environment dependency (npm on PATH) that could flake in minimal CI images.

Additive and scoped: --executor is undefined on plain init (no behavior change), lib/onboard.mjs is a leaf module (no import cycle with store.mjs), `chalk agents` without --claude is unchanged, and requiredAt:['per-task'] is honored by the existing cadence resolver in lib/config.mjs so the wired review actually gates. Reverting the change fails multiple locked tests (share/ missing, --executor refused), so the suite is non-vacuous. The .chalk/ state flips (prior tasks → done) are chalk-managed bookkeeping, not hand-edits of this task's state.

title: feat: doctor for strangers — per-OS gh install hints, optional-executor framing, --json output, unused-gates nudge

acceptanceCriteria.text: missing gh/git produce per-OS copy-paste install hints (installHint exported+pure); gh is scoped 'needed for the issue/PR pipeline, NOT for the local loop'

This is testable today — inject github.command pointing at a nonexistent binary via the existing stubStore and assert the toolchain fail message. If the scoping phrase or the installHint interpolation were reverted, the locked suite would still pass — a vacuous pass under the break-it rule.

For a custom gh command (protocol.github.command = 'node /path/fake-gh.mjs' — a config the test file itself acknowledges at doctor.test.mjs:37), installHint returns '', so the doctor fail line renders as '<cmd> not found on PATH —  (needed for the issue/PR pipeline...)' with a dangling em-dash and no fix. The 'copy-paste fix, not a search' goal is unmet exactly when the binary name is nonstandard; the message should omit the '—' or fall back to a generic hint.

Criterion 4 says the exit code is 'preserved', but only the failing case (exit 2) is tested. The READY→exit 0 path under --json is untested, and no test asserts json/non-json exit-code parity; process.exit(fails ? 2 : 0) inside the json branch (bin/chalk.mjs:724) could regress to unconditional 0 or 2 without any test failing.

Criterion 3 says the nudge names 'exactly' the OFF levers and 'armed levers drop out', but only breakTest arming is tested. Arming mutation / held-out / review-cadence / plan.required dropping out, and the all-armed case (no info line at all), are unasserted — e.g. the plan-approval condition uses proto.plan?.required, and a truthiness bug there (e.g. checking proto.plan instead) would go uncaught.

runDoctor now emits a new 'info' level. The only other consumer (lib/autopilot.mjs:29) filters strictly on level === 'fail', so autopilot gating is unaffected, and the CLI icon map includes 'info' — no regression found, but no test pins that the info nudge never influences the doctor exit code or the autopilot NOT-READY decision.

Criterion 3 ('names exactly the levers that are OFF') is pinned via the all-off, one-armed (breakTest), and all-armed cases, but mutation/held-out/review/plan-approval are never individually armed — a wiring bug that fails to drop exactly one of those four from the nudge would go undetected.

The info-level nudge and the new '·' info icon are only asserted through --json payloads; pretty-mode rendering of info lines (icon map now includes 'info' where it previously did not) is untested, so a regression there would print 'undefined' silently.

installHint defaults platform to process.platform inside runDoctor's withHint, so the doctor line's hint is host-dependent; tests only assert the generic /install:/ marker in wiring, which is acceptable but means per-OS wiring (vs. the pure function) is unverified — mitigated by the pure-function per-OS unit test.
